summary: Fixed two issues in the entry-signal engine. (1) Bar fetch (scripts/compute_indicators.py fetch_5min_bars + fetch_daily_bars) was passing Alpaca's `limit`, which returns the OLDEST bars in the window — so RSI/EMA/MACD/EMA-200 and watchlist `close` were computed off days-stale bars (NVDA was on last Friday's data). Now end-anchored: fetches the full window / tails the dailies, so indicators use current bars. (2) rel_vol redefined as time-of-day RVOL (lib/indicators.time_of_day_rvol): today's cumulative volume vs the average cumulative volume up to the same time of day over the prior RVOL_LOOKBACK_DAYS (14) sessions — feed-agnostic, fixing the structurally-low IEX readings that were the binding constraint rejecting ~23/25 names. Verified live: NVDA 1.08, AAPL 1.52, MBUU 1.72, MCY 0.12 (vs old broken 0.196 / 2.37). VWAP now resets per session. Added config INDICATOR_INTRADAY_LOOKBACK_DAYS=20, RVOL_LOOKBACK_DAYS=14. Legacy compute_relative_volume / apply_all_intraday signatures kept for back-compat; all 36 tests pass. No trade decisions made this run.

Discovered an untracked -18 MPC SHORT in the live Alpaca account (not in positions.json). Root cause: the original MPC long's protective stop FILLED at 2026-06-04 16:38:02Z (18 @ 268.977778), closing the long ~1 min after the 16:37 entry, but that fill was never reconciled into positions.json. At EOD the review agent read the stale 'open long' state and sold 18 @ 268.00 to 'close' it -- with no long remaining, that opened an unintended -18 short, which then sat UNPROTECTED (no stop) overnight and into today. Action taken: bought-to-cover 18 @ 266.57 at ~10:20 ET today; account now FLAT with no open orders. Corrected P&L: true long result -12.10 (stop), accidental short +25.74 (MPC kept falling), net MPC episode +13.64 (vs the -29.70 the EOD journal had logged). Appended RECONCILE + two corrective EXIT records to trade_log.jsonl. UNDERLYING BUG to fix in code: place_order/EOD-close logic does not detect when a broker-side stop order self-fills, so positions.json drifts out of sync with the broker and a subsequent 'close' can flip flat->short. Recommend reconciling positions.json against tc.get_all_positions() before any close, and verifying side/qty before submitting sells.

observations: Day 10. The long-awaited green-day lean-in (WDC, a clean 7/7 midday qualifier) was stopped out 98 seconds after the 12:35 PM entry -- filled $561.41 vs $561.53 stop, -$18.58/-0.41%, the entire day P&L while SPY gained +0.39% (alpha -0.41%). Root cause was NOT thesis failure but the recurring low-ATR hair-trigger: WDC atr_pct ~0.17% put the 1.5xATR stop only ~0.4%/$2.20 below entry, inside routine noise -- the THIRD such minutes-after-entry scratch (MPC, CIEN, WDC), now a clear repeating mechanical pattern that should push entry selection to widen the stop floor or skip sub-0.3%-ATR names. The fill-reconciliation bug also bit a 3rd time: the stop self-filled but was never logged, so I reconstructed the EXIT from Alpaca order history before update_memory; place_order correctly refused to sell into the already-flat broker. Book ends flat, no overnight holds; cumulative alpha still +2.27pts vs SPY but now entirely cash-preservation, not execution.

weekly_observations: Week 2 of live trading (sessions 6-10) was a choppy risk-off-into-recovery tape: SPY round-tripped from +0.29% Mon to -1.37% Wed, V-recovered +1.77% Thu and closed +0.39% Fri (+0.34% net), with VIX easing back under 20 only on Friday. Bull took just 2 entries and lost both (0% win rate, -01.88, -0.10% week, -0.44% vs SPY): CIEN (-3.30) was a textbook failed-bounce buy on a red ~-4% gap-down name that passed the mechanical gates but never should have been entered without a green/above-VWAP filter, and WDC (-8.58) was the third low-ATR hair-trigger scratch in four lifetime trades -- stopped 98s after entry because a 1.5xATR stop on a ~0.17% ATR name sits inside routine noise. The only thing that worked was cash discipline: three clean no-trade days preserved capital through the unwind and kept cumulative alpha positive (+2.27pts vs SPY), but that edge is now entirely cash-preservation, NOT execution, and Thursday proved the cost -- Bull sat out the +1.77% rip. Priorities for Mon 06-15: (1) refuse intraday-bounce buys unless the name is green/above-prev-close/above-VWAP at entry; (2) impose a minimum stop-distance floor of max(1.5xATR, ~0.6-0.8% of price) or skip/size-down any sub-0.30% atr_pct name -- the too-tight-stop pattern is now the single most repeated, most costly mechanical failure on the record; (3) the fill-reconciliation code bug bit a 3rd time (CIEN+WDC stop fills went unlogged and had to be reconstructed from broker history) and remains unfixed -- it should be treated as a real engineering task, and any agent must reconcile broker order history into trade_log before update_memory.

observations: Day 11. First genuine signal-driven winning ENTRY on record: SNDK (atr%0.338, rsi 59, storage-cluster leader) extended +2.58% -- the +2 ATR partial banked +52.99 and the last share trailed out at 2092.00 for +1.73% (round-trip +88.47), so it logs as 2 winning exits but is really one good entry. Offset by TSEM (atr%0.285) noise-stopping 33s after entry DESPITE the widened 0.70% floor (5th low-ATR hair-trigger), so the day netted only +34.07/+0.034% while SPY ran +1.73% (alpha -1.69%, 2nd straight green-day laggard). Book ended FLAT -- SNDK final share stopped at 1:13 PM ET, nothing to carry. The fill-reconciliation bug bit a 5th time (SNDK stop self-fill unlogged; reconstructed from broker history before update_memory). Takeaway: the edge lives in higher-ATR (>0.30%) trending leaders where the partial logic can fire; sub-0.30%-ATR names keep noise-stopping and the 0.70% floor is not enough. Cumulative alpha cushion has collapsed to ~+0.46pts vs SPY -- fixing the stop floor is the highest-leverage change before alpha flips negative.
